paragraph: The Ramsey single-family market is running hot right now. Fifty-three homes sold over seven months at a median of $940,000, with over half of those sales closing above asking price and a median days on market of 17 days. The sale-to-list ratio sitting at 1.02 confirms that sellers are dictating terms here, particularly on homes priced competitively in the $800,000 to $1.2 million range where you'll see multiple offer situations. By contrast, the condo and townhouse market is even stronger, with 31 sales at a median of $590,000 and nearly 78 percent of homes selling above list price. These properties are moving faster, spending a median of 12 days on market before going under contract. The sale-to-list ratio of 1.06 and the speed at which inventory turns over suggest condo buyers are facing real competition. If you're weighing a single-family home against a condo in Ramsey, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family or the lower maintenance lifestyle and entry point of a condo.
